cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out what the main topic of the passage is. Let me start by reading through the text again to make sure I understand it properly. The passage begins by talking about how both men and women are surrounded by magazines and websites that focus on dieting, exercise, fashion, and hairstyles. It mentions that in the past, men only had to worry about wearing a nice suit to be considered smart and handsome, but times have changed. Then it references a survey by the University of the West of England (UWE) that found 80% of men are unhappy with their bodies, specifically pointing out issues like beer bellies and lack of muscles. The text suggests that this increased concern about body image might be due to the influence of TV and magazine role models, which set a standard for the "perfect" body.
